title: Smoky Middle Eastern Poppyseed‑Roasted Chicken Thighs
description: Crispy, aromatic chicken thighs coated with ground poppy seeds and smoked spices, finished in a tandoor‑style oven for a hearty solo dinner.

ingredients.name: ground poppy seeds
ingredients.notes: toasting in a dry skillet first enhances flavor

instructions: Preheat the oven to 220 °C (425 °F) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper or a wire rack.
In a small skillet, toast the poppy seeds over medium heat for 2‑3 minutes until fragrant; set aside to cool.
Combine the toasted poppy seeds, smoked paprika, cumin, sumac, garlic powder, salt, pepper, and lemon zest in a bowl to create the spice paste.
Pat the chicken thighs dry with paper towels; drizzle 1 tbsp of olive oil over the thighs and rub the spice paste all over them, ensuring an even coating.
Arrange the coated thighs skin side up on the prepared baking sheet, leaving space between each piece.
Bake in the preheated oven for 35 minutes, then flip the thighs and continue roasting for an additional 10‑12 minutes until the skin is crisp and the internal temperature reaches 75 °C (165 °F).
Remove the chicken from the oven and let it rest on a cutting board for 5 minutes to allow juices to redistribute.
Serve the roasted thighs with a side of fresh lemon wedges for squeezing and optionally a simple cucumber‑yogurt salad for a Mediterranean touch.
If desired, drizzle a little extra olive oil and sprinkle additional sumac over the finished dish for brightness.
Enjoy the smoky, poppy‑seed‑infused flavors that pair wonderfully with a light flatbread or salad.
